commands: WebSearch: dark photon kinetic mixing constraints sub-GeV beam dump NA64 E137 invisible decay 2024 review
WebSearch: dark sector Delta Neff BBN supercooled phase transition MeV reheating PTA NANOGrav xi=1
WebSearch: SN1987A dark photon bound 100-700 MeV kinetic mixing epsilon excluded band trapping free-streaming
WebFetch: https://arxiv.org/html/2502.19478 (Balan et al., same conformal dark U(1) + sub-GeV DM)
WebFetch: https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.11619 (Goncalves et al., conformal dark sector NANOGrav)
warnings: The PTA/FOPT handoffs fix epsilon~0 and lambda_HPhi~0 for the GW computation, but the xi=1 (T_dark=T_vis) thermalization assumption that enters g_* and the GW redshift REQUIRES a nonzero portal (kinetic mixing or Higgs portal). epsilon and lambda_HPhi are therefore NOT free to be exactly zero on the cosmology side; this is an internal tension flagged by the critic and must be resolved by computing a thermalization/decay window for epsilon.

No predicted value of epsilon, no dark photon decay width/lifetime, no Delta Neff, and no relic/freeze-out yield are present in any upstream handoff. Most cosmological and laboratory constraints therefore CANNOT yet be applied to a point; they are mapped to required missing calculations.

Dark photon mass in the 1-sigma region is m_A' ~ 0.32-0.67 GeV, which is ABOVE the SN1987A energy-loss reach (m_A' < ~0.1 GeV, 1611.03864); SN1987A is therefore not applicable to the preferred-region dark photon.

Quantitative epsilon-vs-mass bounds quoted from the 2026 dark-photon review (2603.08430) were read from a model summary of the PDF, not from a numerical table; treat the epsilon numbers as order-of-magnitude until cross-checked against darkcast/the published exclusion plots.
approximations: Visible-decay collider/beam-dump epsilon bounds at 0.3-0.7 GeV taken as order-of-magnitude bands (epsilon ~ 1e-3 down to ~1e-5) from the 2026 dark-photon review; not recast onto the exact model decay assumptions.
Dark Higgs (scalon) decay width formula and lifetime example (tau_phi ~ 2500 s at kappa=1e-4) taken from 2502.19478 for nearby parameters; not recomputed for the exact 1-sigma point.
BBN reheating bound Treh > ~3 MeV adopted as the standard MeV-reheating limit (2502.19478, AlterBBN); the preferred-region Treh ~ 60-100 MeV comfortably exceeds it.

m_rho_scalon_GeV.note: light scalon; coeff sqrt(3 g_D^4/(8 pi^2)) ~ 0.0175-0.0193 over the g_D band

Treh_GeV.note: reheating temperature from FOPT handoff in the overlapping v,g_D window

notes: Independent params v and g_D from PTArcade NG15 ceffyl 68% credible region (handoff_pta.json bayes_estimates.credible_regions.sigma_1).
Dark sector relativistic dof at the transition: A' (3) + scalon rho (1); xi=1 assumed.
epsilon (kinetic mixing) and lambda_HPhi (Higgs portal) are fixed ~0 for the GW dynamics but must be nonzero for thermalization/decay (cosmology side).

constraints.name: BBN bound on MeV-scale reheating temperature
constraints.description: A FOPT completing near the MeV scale must reheat the SM plasma above the neutrino-decoupling / BBN temperature so that SM neutrinos re-equilibrate and light-element abundances are preserved. Standard conservative limit Treh > ~3 MeV (model-dependent ~1-4 MeV).

conventions.Treh: reheating temperature after FOPT completion (visible-sector if entropy is shared)

constraints.applicability: Treh ~ 0.06-0.10 GeV (60-100 MeV) across the 1-sigma region comfortably exceeds the ~3 MeV BBN floor; the preferred region is NOT excluded by this bound.
applicability_notes: Caveat: the >3 MeV floor assumes the released latent heat reaches the SM bath; in a fully secluded sector with epsilon~0 the SM is not reheated and the bound application changes. Whether SM reheating occurs depends on the (currently unspecified) portal.
Treh in this model is in GeV; bound quoted in MeV. Conversion explicit: 0.06 GeV = 60 MeV >> 3 MeV.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Delta Neff from dark radiation (CMB + BBN)
constraints.description: A thermalized dark sector (xi=1) with extra relativistic dof (A':3, scalon:1) contributes Delta Neff. Planck+BAO require Neff = 2.99 +/- 0.17, i.e. Delta Neff <~ 0.3-0.4. The contribution depends on when the dark dof become non-relativistic relative to neutrino decoupling and on whether the dark entropy is transferred to neutrinos/photons via a portal.
required_inputs: portal (epsilon or lambda_HPhi) for entropy transfer

conventions.Delta Neff: extra effective neutrino species at CMB; relate to dark T ratio via 7/8(4/11)^(4/3) normalization

constraints.applicability: Potentially the most constraining bound on the xi=1 assumption. A fully thermalized dark photon+scalon decoupling while relativistic can give Delta Neff well above 0.3 unless the dark dof become non-relativistic and/or dump their entropy into the SM before neutrino decoupling. Cannot be applied without computing Delta Neff for the preferred region.

tested_subregion.note: entire 1-sigma region pending Delta Neff computation

applicability_notes: m_A' ~ 0.3-0.7 GeV and m_rho ~ 11-24 MeV: A' is non-relativistic well before BBN, but the light scalon (~20 MeV) is near the neutrino-decoupling scale (~2 MeV) only marginally above it, so its late entropy injection / Delta Neff must be evaluated.
Whether the dark entropy heats neutrinos or photons (sign and size of Delta Neff) depends on the portal channel, which is unspecified.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Thermalization / dark-visible equilibrium (justification of xi=1)
constraints.description: xi=1 (T_dark=T_vis) requires the dark and visible sectors to have been in thermal contact. With lambda_HPhi << 1e-10 (required by conformality) the only viable bridge is kinetic mixing epsilon. A minimum epsilon is needed for the dark photon to thermalize with the SM (Gamma > H) at the relevant temperature.

coupling_range.epsilon_needed: lower bound to be computed; literature window 1e-7 < epsilon < 1e-3 for nearby parameters (2502.19478)

required_observables: thermalization rate Gamma(epsilon) vs Hubble H
constraints.applicability: The xi=1 assumption that enters g_* and the GW redshift is not self-consistent at epsilon=0. A nonzero epsilon must be present; the minimum epsilon for thermalization must be computed and intersected with laboratory and SN bounds. This is an internal-consistency constraint, not an exclusion.

tested_subregion.note: applies to whole region via the fixed xi=1 assumption

applicability_notes: Critic handoff explicitly flags the tension between xi=1 and negligible epsilon/lambda_HPhi.
Literature (2502.19478) uses kappa(=epsilon) in [1e-7, 1e-3] for thermalization + prompt A'->e+e- decay; this window must be checked against the visible-decay collider/beam-dump bounds (C04-C06).

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Visible-decay collider searches on dark photon (BaBar, KLOE, LHCb, Belle II)
constraints.description: A GeV-scale dark photon mixing with the SM photon decays visibly (A'->e+e-, mu+mu-, hadrons) and is searched for in resonance/displaced-vertex modes. For m_A' ~ 0.3-0.7 GeV, prompt visible searches exclude roughly epsilon ~ 1e-3 down to ~few x 1e-4.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' (visible BR-weighted production cross section)

conventions.epsilon: standard hypercharge/photon kinetic mixing; production assumes A' couples to SM EM current with strength epsilon e

constraints.applicability: Applies only if A' decays visibly inside the detector (prompt), i.e. for the upper part of the thermalization epsilon window. Cannot be applied without the predicted epsilon and the visible/invisible branching split (A'->dark states is forbidden here since m_A' < 2 m_rho? check: m_A'~0.3-0.7 GeV vs 2 m_rho ~ 0.02-0.05 GeV, so A'->rho rho is OPEN -> invisible/dark decays compete).
applicability_notes: Crucially, 2 m_rho ~ 21-49 MeV << m_A' ~ 311-673 MeV, so the dark decay A'->rho+rho (or A'->dark states) is kinematically OPEN and can dominate, making A' largely INVISIBLE to visible-decay searches. The visible/invisible branching ratio must be computed before applying C04.
epsilon ranges are order-of-magnitude from 2603.08430; recast against darkcast needed.

constraints.name: Electron beam-dump constraints (E137, E141, E774, NA64-visible)
constraints.description: For small epsilon the dark photon is long-lived and can be produced in beam dumps and decay visibly downstream. E137/E141/E774 exclude a band of small epsilon (~1e-7 to ~1e-4) for sub-GeV masses, complementary to collider bounds.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' for long-lived visibly-decaying A'

conventions.epsilon: standard photon kinetic mixing; bound depends on decay length matching dump-to-detector geometry

constraints.applicability: Applies only in the small-epsilon, long-lived, visible-decay corner AND only if A'->visible has appreciable BR. Since A'->dark (rho rho) is open, the visible BR may be suppressed, weakening beam-dump reach. Cannot apply without epsilon and the branching split.
applicability_notes: Beam-dump bounds weaken substantially if A' decays invisibly to dark states (open here).
epsilon band is order-of-magnitude; recast needed.

source.title: Strong Constraints on Sub-GeV Dark Sectors from SLAC Beam Dump E137

constraints.name: Invisible / missing-energy dark photon searches (NA64-invisible, BaBar mono-photon)
constraints.description: If the dark photon decays predominantly to dark states (here A'->rho rho is open), missing-energy searches (NA64 invisible, BaBar single-photon) constrain epsilon as a function of m_A'. Typical reach epsilon ~ 1e-4 to 1e-3 for sub-GeV invisible A'.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' for invisibly-decaying A'

conventions.epsilon: standard photon kinetic mixing; missing-energy bound assumes BR(A'->invisible)~1

constraints.applicability: Likely the most relevant collider-class bound IF A' is dominantly invisible (A'->rho rho open). Requires the predicted epsilon and the invisible branching ratio. Not yet applicable.
applicability_notes: Because 2 m_rho << m_A', the invisible (dark) channel is open; this is the more physically relevant collider category for this model rather than visible-decay searches.
rho itself eventually decays back to SM via the epsilon-induced loop, but on cosmological timescales, so it is detector-invisible.

constraints.name: SN1987A energy-loss bound on dark photon
constraints.description: Light dark photons produced in the SN1987A core can free-stream out and shorten the neutrino burst, excluding epsilon ~ 1e-10 to 1e-6 for m_A' below ~100 MeV.
constraints.applicability: NOT APPLICABLE: the preferred-region dark photon mass m_A' ~ 0.31-0.67 GeV lies ABOVE the SN1987A reach (m_A' < ~0.1 GeV). The SN core temperature (~30 MeV) cannot copiously produce a 0.3-0.7 GeV dark photon.
applicability_notes: 1611.03864 explicitly limits its analysis to m_A' < ~100 MeV.
Do not apply SN1987A to this region from mass alone; it is out of the production-kinematics regime.

source.title: Revisiting Supernova 1987A Constraints on Dark Photons

constraints.name: Light scalon (dark Higgs) decay and late energy injection (BBN/CMB)
constraints.description: The scalon rho (m_rho ~ 11-24 MeV) decays only via epsilon-suppressed loops to SM fermions, with a long lifetime (e.g. tau_phi ~ 2500 s at kappa=1e-4 for nearby parameters). A long-lived MeV-scale relic that decays during/after BBN can inject electromagnetic/hadronic energy and dissociate light nuclei or distort the CMB.
constraints.applicability: Potentially constraining: an MeV scalon with tau >> 1 s decaying electromagnetically can violate BBN/CMB energy-injection limits unless its abundance is tiny or it decays fast enough. Requires computing the scalon width, lifetime and freeze-out yield for the preferred region. m_rho < 2 m_e? No: m_rho ~ 11-24 MeV > 2 m_e (1.022 MeV), so rho->e+e- is open; rho->mu+mu- and hadrons closed.
applicability_notes: rho is the lightest dark state and, with epsilon~0, would be cosmologically stable -> overclosure / extra dark matter. With nonzero epsilon it decays to e+e-; the lifetime sets whether it injects energy during BBN (t~1-1e3 s) or recombination.
This couples directly to C02 (Delta Neff) and to the dark-matter relic question; the scalon abundance must be tracked.

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Scalon / dark-state relic overabundance (secluded sector)
constraints.description: In the strictly secluded limit (epsilon=0), the lightest dark state (scalon rho) is stable and its thermal relic abundance could overclose the Universe or exceed Omega_DM. Whether this is a constraint depends on the freeze-out/annihilation cross section (rho rho -> A' A' etc.).
constraints.applicability: Cannot be applied without a relic-abundance calculation. If rho is stable (epsilon=0) it must not exceed Omega_DM h^2 ~ 0.12; if it decays (epsilon nonzero) C08 applies instead. The minimal DM-free model studied here has no dedicated DM candidate, so the scalon fate is an open cosmology question.
applicability_notes: The user's model is the DM-free version of 2502.19478; without a DM fermion the scalon is the lightest dark state and its cosmological fate (stable relic vs decay) is unspecified.
Coupled to C03 (epsilon needed) and C08 (decay).

source.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector

constraints.name: Higgs portal coupling / conformality and Higgs invisible width
constraints.description: The Higgs portal lambda_HPhi |H|^2|Phi|^2 must remain << 1e-10 to preserve classical scale invariance (avoid an induced tree mass term). Such a tiny coupling is far below any Higgs invisible-width or direct collider sensitivity, so the Higgs invisible-width bound is automatically satisfied.
constraints.applicability: The conformality requirement lambda_HPhi << 1e-10 is FAR stronger than any collider Higgs-portal bound (current BR(h->inv) < ~0.1 probes lambda_HPhi ~ O(0.01)). The model assumption therefore trivially satisfies the Higgs invisible-width constraint. The binding constraint here is theoretical (conformality), not experimental.
applicability_notes: This is a self-consistency / theory constraint that the FOPT analysis already relies on; recorded for completeness.
If lambda_HPhi were the thermalization bridge instead of epsilon, it could not be (too small); hence epsilon is the required portal (see C03).

source.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data

constraints.name: Perturbativity / theory bound on g_D and lambda_Phi
constraints.description: Perturbative validity of the one-loop CW + thermal effective potential requires g_D, |lambda_Phi| sufficiently below the strong-coupling regime. The preferred g_D ~ 0.51-0.55 gives g_D^2/(4 pi) ~ 0.02, well within perturbativity.
constraints.observable: alpha_D = g_D^2/(4 pi), loop expansion parameter
constraints.applicability: g_D ~ 0.51-0.55 => alpha_D ~ 0.021-0.024 << 1. Perturbativity is satisfied across the entire 1-sigma region. NOT excluded.
applicability_notes: Separately, the GW spectrum carries an order-of-magnitude gauge-dependence theory uncertainty (1707.06765) that is NOT an exclusion but should be propagated; recorded in warnings of upstream handoffs.

source.title: On gauge dependence of gravitational waves from a FOPT in classical scale-invariant U(1)' models

constraints.name: Dark photon direct detection (terrestrial)
constraints.description: Direct-detection / electron-recoil experiments (SENSEI, DAMIC-M, XENON) constrain kinetically-mixed dark photons mainly as a dark-matter candidate or via absorption. Here A' is unstable (decays to dark/SM states) and is not the relic DM, so absorption-DM limits do not apply.
constraints.observable: DM-electron / DM-nucleon scattering or A' absorption
constraints.applicability: NOT APPLICABLE: the dark photon in this model is a short-lived FOPT-sector mediator, not a relic dark-matter candidate, so dark-photon-DM direct-detection/absorption limits do not constrain it. (A separate scalon-relic question is covered by C09.)

tested_subregion.verdict: not applicable - A' is not DM

applicability_notes: Do not apply DM direct-detection bounds from mass alone; the relic-abundance / stability assumptions are not met for A'.

missing_calculations.observable: predicted/required kinetic mixing epsilon and minimum epsilon for xi=1 thermalization
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: new: dark_portal_thermalization.py (compute Gamma_thermal(epsilon,T) vs H(T); solve for epsilon_min giving xi=1)
validation_checks: consistency with the GW-side assumption that epsilon is negligible for the potential (epsilon^2 corrections << 1)

missing_calculations.observable: dark photon decay width, lifetime and visible/invisible branching ratios (A'->e+e-, A'->rho rho/dark)
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: new: dark_photon_decay.py (partial widths to e+e-, hadrons, and dark rho rho; decay length)
validation_checks: A'->rho rho open since 2 m_rho << m_A'
compare A' decay length to beam-dump geometry and to t_BBN

missing_calculations.observable: Delta Neff from the thermalized dark sector at CMB and BBN
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: new: dark_Neff.py (track dark dof g_*dark(T), decoupling, entropy ratio; map to Delta Neff)

missing_calculations.observable: scalon (rho) relic yield, lifetime and BBN/CMB energy-injection
required_inputs: annihilation cross section rho rho -> A' A'
missing_calculations.suggested_backend_module: new: scalon_relic_decay.py (freeze-out yield + epsilon-loop decay width to e+e-; injection epoch)
validation_checks: tau_rho vs t_BBN (~1-1e3 s) and t_rec
